title: Feedback Loop Simulator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Biology
description: Interactive p5.js simulation comparing how negative and positive biological feedback loops respond to perturbations.

learningObjectives: Differentiate balancing (negative) and reinforcing (positive) feedback loops by observing the time-series response.
Identify the role of the stimulus, detector, control center, and effector within a feedback circuit.
Predict whether a biological example will return to its set point or diverge after a disturbance.

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive exploration with prediction, perturbation, and comparison tasks
keyQuestions: What visual evidence shows the loop returning to its set point?
How does the reinforcing loop continue to grow without intervention?
commonMisconceptions: Positive feedback always represents a desirable outcome
Detectors only exist in negative feedback circuits
assessmentOpportunities: Learners explain how a chosen example responds after clicking Apply Perturbation
Students map each diagram node to a structure in the selected scenario
